channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Georgian PM praises country's protest crackdown despite US condemnation
item.description: Georgia, which has been criticized for using "excessive force" to crack down on anti-government protests, has become increasingly authoritarian, according to Western governments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Negotiators fail to reach an agreement on a plastic pollution treaty. Talks to resume next year
item.description: Negotiators working on a treaty to address the global crisis of plastic pollution won’t reach an agreement in South Korea and are making plans to resume the talks next year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Over 40 people hospitalized in Georgia during protests over the suspension of EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — A third night of protests in the Georgian capital against the government’s decision to suspend negotiations to join the European Union left 44 people hospitalized, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrian military launches counterattacks in an attempt to halt insurgents' surprise advance
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian military rushed reinforcements to the country's northwest and launched airstrikes on a major city Sunday in an attempt to push back insurgents who seized the country's largest city of Aleppo in a surprise offensive in recent days.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social democrats gain and incumbents are punished in Iceland's election
item.description: REYKJAVIK, Iceland (AP) — Voters in Iceland joined a global trend of punishing incumbents in a parliamentary election, with a center-left party winning the largest share of votes in the North Atlantic island nation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ireland election: Incumbent center-right parties likely to retain power, but left-wing Sinn Fein holds water
item.description: Ireland's election results have yet to be finalized, but exit polling suggests a coalition government between the center-right Fine Gael and Fianna Fáil.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ireland's incumbent parties look likely to hang on to power after a fractured election
item.description: Results from Ireland's election suggest the two dominant center-right parties look likely to form a new government.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Peter Morrison, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Plastic treaty talks draw to a close with production limits still under debate
item.description: BUSAN, South Korea (AP) — Negotiations on a treaty to end plastic pollution are drawing to a close Sunday as nations continue debating whether part of that should be putting limits on plastic production.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN halts aid shipments through Gaza's main crossing after looting. It blames the crisis on Israel
item.description: The U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees says it is halting aid deliveries through the main cargo crossing into Gaza because of the threat of armed gangs who have looted recent convoys.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A landmark climate change case will open at the top UN court as island nations fear rising seas
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The top United Nations court will take up the largest case in its history on Monday, when it opens two weeks of hearings into what countries worldwide are legally required to do to combat climate change and help vulnerable nations fight its devastating impact.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Iceland votes for a new parliament after political disagreements force an early election
item.description: REYKJAVIK, Iceland (AP) — Icelanders voted to elect a new parliament Saturday after disagreements over immigration, energy policy and the economy forced Prime Minister Bjarni Benediktsson to pull the plug on his coalition government and call an early election.
item.dc:creator: MARCO DI MARCO and DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Israeli strike in Gaza kills World Central Kitchen workers. Israel says 1 was an Oct. 7 attacker
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a car in the Gaza Strip on Saturday killed five people, including employees of World Central Kitchen.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, TIA GOLDENBERG and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy says NATO offer for Ukraine-controlled territory could end 'hot stage' of war
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An offer of NATO membership to territory under Kyiv’s control would end “the hot stage of the war” in Ukraine, but any proposal to join the military alliance should be extended to all parts of the country that fall under internationally recognized borders, President Volodymyr Ze
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump lauds 'productive' meeting with Trudeau amid tension over tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump confirmed his Friday meeting with Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling the conversation "productive.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, The Hill

item.title: Israeli military says Hezbollah is smuggling weapons into Lebanon during ceasefire
item.description: Hezbollah terrorists reportedly are smuggling weapons across Lebanon's borders despite an ongoing ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Romania's parliamentary vote risks being overshadowed by presidential race chaos
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Hundreds of protesters gathered in Bucharest after a far-right populist unexpectedly won the first round of a presidential race, plunging Romania into turmoil just days before the country holds parliamentary elections. “Better to be dead than a fascist,” one placard reads.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about shock offensive on Syria's largest city
item.description: Syrian rebels against the country's President Bashar Al-Assad advanced toward one of the largest cities in the country — the first time since 2016 — following a shock offensive launched by insurgents on Wednesday, the Associated Press reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: In a shock offensive, insurgents breach Syria's largest city for the first time since 2016
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Insurgents breached Syria’s largest city Friday and clashed with government forces for the first time since 2016, according to a war monitor and fighters, in a surprise attack that sent residents fleeing and added fresh uncertainty to a region reeling from multiple wars.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Enraged passenger unloads flurry of punches on airport worker, wild video shows
item.description: A furious airline passenger was captured on dramatic video unleashing a flurry of punches on an airport worker over an apparent check-in delay dispute.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nigeria confirms that at least 27 people died and more than 100 are missing after boat capsized
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — At least 27 people died and more than 100, mostly women, were missing on Friday, after a boat transporting them to a food market capsized along the River Niger in northern Nigeria, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK lawmakers vote in favor of bill legalizing assisted dying
item.description: Lawmakers in Britain gave initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives in England and Wales in a vote in the House of Commons on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stowaway who boarded New York-to-Paris flight will be sent back to US
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Authorities are investigating after a Russian national who did not have a ticket boarded a Paris-bound Delta flight at New York's John F. Kennedy International Airport this week and was arrested when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel cease-fire deal has critics worried over Biden, Obama officials looking to stymie incoming Trump admin
item.description: After 14 months of war between Israel and Hezbollah, a cease-fire went into effect this week amid allegations that the truce happened due to pressure put on Israel despite denials from the administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Hiker accidentally uncovers 280-million-year-old footprints in Italy
item.description: Hikers were responsible for the discovery of what scientists believe to be a prehistoric ecosystem. The first sign of the ecosystem discovered by the hikers were footprints.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada is already examining tariffs on certain US items following Trump's tariff threat
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada is already examining possible retaliatory tariffs on certain items from the United States should President-elect Donald Trump follow through on his threat to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products, a senior official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: World's oldest man has died in England at 112
item.description: John Alfred Tinniswood was born a few months after the sinking of the Titanic and lived through two world wars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel launches new airstrikes on Lebanon as leaders draw closer to a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel's military launched airstrikes across Lebanon on Monday, unleashing explosions throughout the country and killing at least 31 while Israeli leaders appeared to be closing in on a negotiated ceasefire with the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pledges Mexico, Canada, China tariffs over border, fentanyl
item.description: "Thousands of people are pouring through Mexico and Canada, bringing Crime and Drugs at levels never seen before," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Amelia Earhart’s plane photo not of the plane, company says
item.description: “After 11 months the waiting has finally ended and unfortunately our target was not Amelia's Electra 10E," the exploration company said.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Two senators urge FIFA not to pick Saudi Arabia as 2034 host
item.description: Democrats Ron Wyden of Oregon and Dick Durbin of Illinois have told FIFA president Gianni Infantino in a letter “we urge you to seek out a host country with a record of upholding human rights.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Drones detected near 3 US bases in England last week: Air Force
item.description: Between Wednesday and Friday, multiple unidentified drones were detected near RAF Lakenheath, RAF Mildenhall and RAF Feltwell.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation
